items.title: Interest Rate Cuts 'On Hold... If There Are Any At All In 2025' After December's Sticky Consumer Inflation Report: Economists
items.author: Erica Kollmann
items.description: Economists believe that interest rate cuts may be on hold or even unlikely in 2025 due to persistent inflation, as indicated by the latest December CPI report. The Federal Reserve will consider this data in its upcoming interest rate decision.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The SPDR S&P 500 ETF, which tracks the S&P 500 index, opened higher on the news, indicating a positive market reaction.
The Invesco QQQ Trust, which tracks the Nasdaq 100 index, also opened higher, suggesting a positive market sentiment.

items.title: Stocks Rise On Core Inflation Slowdown, Banks Rally After Strong Q4 Earnings, Treasury Yields Plummet
items.author: Piero Cingari
items.description: Stocks rallied as December inflation data showed underlying price pressures slowing, reducing fears of a prolonged pause in interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve. Major banks reported strong Q4 earnings, boosting the financial sector.

items.title: 5 Monster Stocks to Hold for the Next 5 Years (and Ideally Beyond) -- Including Nvidia
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: The article highlights five stocks that have shown exceptional growth over the past decade, including Nvidia, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, Arista Networks, and Axon Enterprise. It also recommends the SPDR S&P 500 ETF as a less volatile approach to building wealth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Nvidia has been an exceptional performer, averaging annual gains of 87% over the past five years and 75% over the past decade. The article suggests that it still doesn't seem overvalued relative to past years.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing is the world's biggest contract semiconductor chipmaker, making Nvidia chips and many others. It has grown powerfully in recent years, averaging annual gains of 25.6% over the past decade.

Arista Networks is a cloud networking specialist that has averaged annual gains of about 40% over the past decade. However, its stock is currently richly valued, so the article suggests it is more of a hold than a buy for more cautious investors.

Axon Enterprise, a specialist in law enforcement technology, has averaged annual gains of 36% over the past decade. However, its shares seem overvalued, with a recent forward P/E of 95, well above the five-year average of 71. The article suggests it might be best held and not bought at recent levels.

Axon Enterprise, a specialist in law enforcement technology, has averaged annual gains of 36% over the past decade. However, its shares seem overvalued, with a recent forward P/E of 95, well above the five-year average of 71. The article suggests it might be best held and not bought at recent levels.

The article includes the SPDR S&P 500 ETF as a less volatile approach to building wealth, noting that the S&P 500 has posted lots of double-digit gains in recent years and can still build wealth even if it only averages 6% or 8% returns.

items.title: US Stocks To Open Higher Ahead Of December Inflation Data: All Eyes On Banking Stocks As JPMorgan, Goldman, Citi, Wells Fargo Report Quarterly Earnings
items.author: Rishabh Mishra
items.description: US stock futures are trading higher ahead of the release of December inflation data. Investors are closely watching the banking sector as major banks like JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo report their quarterly earnings.
